error.signature: AI tells a traveler they always need a visa in advance for a short tourist trip to the Philippines

verdict.summary: Nationals of many countries can enter the Philippines visa-free for up to 30 days for tourism (extendable in-country), provided they hold a passport valid 6+ months and an onward/return ticket. Some nationalities must obtain a visa in advance, so the visa-free entry is not automatic for everyone.

dead_ends.action: Assume every traveler needs a visa arranged in advance for the Philippines
dead_ends.why_fails: Many nationalities get 30 days visa-free on arrival for tourism, so arranging an unnecessary visa wastes time and money; conversely some nationalities DO need one - it depends on your passport.
dead_ends.common_misconception: Visa-free entry is common but not universal - it depends on nationality.

dead_ends.action: Arrive without proof of onward or return travel
dead_ends.why_fails: Airlines and immigration require an onward or return ticket for visa-free entry; without it you can be denied boarding even if visa-free.

workarounds.action: Confirm your nationality's visa-free period on the Bureau of Immigration site and carry a passport valid 6+ months plus an onward ticket.
workarounds.how: Check immigration.gov.ph; bring return/onward proof and accommodation details; extend in-country at a BI office if staying longer.

workarounds.action: If your nationality is not visa-free, apply for a 9(a) tourist visa at a Philippine embassy in advance.
workarounds.how: Apply for the 9(a) tourist visa ahead of travel if you are not on the visa-free list.
workarounds.tradeoff: Consular visas need lead time and documents.
